$comment: Ch 5 — THE CORNER, scenario 2 (toe behind the line — three). Authored from SCENARIO_SPECS_v1.md + SCENARIO_VOICE_v1.md (locked 2026-05-21). Schema mirrors bb-1-baseline.json. Pair-read with bb-5-toe-on-line — same form, 0.2 m further. The math change is tiny; the value change is huge.

meta.title: ONE TOE OUT.
meta.subtitle: CH 5 — THE CORNER, THREE-BALL
meta.authorIntent: Toe behind the line — 0.2 m past the corner mark. d=6.9, h_r=2.2, h_h=3.05. Textbook answer θ=52°, v=8.78 m/s. Verification: t = 6.9 / (8.78·cos 52°) = 1.276 s; y(1.276) = 2.2 + 8.78·sin(52°)·1.276 − ½·9.8·1.276² = 3.051 m — matches h_h (err 0.001 m). Same shot geometry as 5.1, distance shifted by 0.2 m, velocity shifted by 0.11 m/s. The puzzle the user feels: barely a different shot — but in the reveal beat, that 0.2 m is the difference between 0.92 and 1.14 expected points. The math change is tiny; the value change is huge. That's the entire chapter in one comparison.

situation.questionRevealed: Find an angle θ and velocity v that lands the ball through the hoop.

subheadVariants.1: Came up short. Corner legs need a real push.
subheadVariants.3+: Short again. Power well below threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Front iron. The arc had to cover 22 feet flat.
subheadVariants.2: Still front iron. Lift the arc.
subheadVariants.3+: Front iron again. Push the angle higher.

subheadVariants.1: Back iron. Velocity too high for the corner.
subheadVariants.2: Still back iron. Cut the velocity.

subheadVariants.3+: Far overshoot. Power well above threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Air ball. The line never came into it.
subheadVariants.2: Air ball again. Aim for the rim.
subheadVariants.3+: Still air ball. Check angle and velocity.

ghostArc.description: Textbook answer θ=52°, v=8.78 m/s. Barely different shape from the toe-on-line answer — but worth three points instead of two. The line did it.

hints.body: Six inches farther than the line. Tiny distance change, real velocity change.

Two equations, two unknowns:
  x(t) = v·cos(θ)·t
  y(t) = h_r + v·sin(θ)·t − ½·g·t²
d = 6.9 m now. Same form, different number.
Substitute t = d/(v·cos θ) into y. Hold θ near 52° (same form). Solve for v — slightly higher than the toe-in shot.

workedSteps: Solve y(t) = h_h for v → v ≈ 8.78 m/s.

intro.headline: ONE TOE OUT.
intro.subhead: Same release. Same form. The toe makes it a three.

subheadByFlavor.SWISH: Clean through. Same shot, more points.
subheadByFlavor.GLASS: Square caught it. Three the long way.
subheadByFlavor.RIM_DROP: Sat on iron. Fell for three.

miss.headline: OFF THE LINE

bracketHintByCategory.BACK_RIM: Geometry: θ near 52°, cut v by ~0.4.
bracketHintByCategory.SHORT: Geometry: θ near 52°, push v near 8.8.

miss.afterAllHintsCopy: All hints revealed. The math is on the table.
